text:
Hello everyone ✌️

The school has a new entity called "Centre d’Appui à la Technologie et à l’Innovation (CATI)", led by Dr. Nourredine Lasla.

The CATI is established by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO). CATI supports innovation, particularly in the area of patents. 

Patents grant inventors exclusive rights to their inventions for up to 20 years, allowing them to protect, license, or sell their ideas. 

They are essential for encouraging innovation and attracting investment. 

You can learn more about CATI's work and intellectual property here: https://www.wipo.int/fr/web/tisc In Algeria, the institution responsible for intellectual property rights is INAPI (Institut National Algérien de la Propriété Industrielle).

Although Dr. Lasla isn't on the server, we will share updates on his behalf. Feel free to contact him for any questions

text:
Hello everyone 👋

This channel is dedicated to introducing you to the exciting world of scientific research and its potential as future outcome or path in your studies. 

Whether you're considering pursuing a PhD or exploring research as a career option, this space will provide valuable guidance.

Here, you'll find resources, insights, and discussions that will help you understand what scientific research entails and how it can enhance your academic journey.

To get started, below is a document outlining the general structure of a research paper. It’s a great way to familiarize yourself with the components of research writing.
raw_content:
Hello everyone 👋

This channel is dedicated to introducing you to the exciting world of scientific research and its potential as future outcome or path in your studies. 

Whether you're considering pursuing a PhD or exploring research as a career option, this space will provide valuable guidance.

Here, you'll find resources, insights, and discussions that will help you understand what scientific research entails and how it can enhance your academic journey.

To get started, below is a document outlining the general structure of a research paper. It’s a great way to familiarize yourself with the components of research writing.

[Attached file: Research-Lab-Reports-IMRAD.pdf]

[Extracted PDF text]
Lab Reports - IMRAD © The Writing Centre, Saint Mary’s University, 2017 This handout is for personal use only. Reproduction prohibited without permission. The purpose of a lab report is to describe the results of an experiment or research study. University lab reports follow the style and format of professional journal articles, which research scientists use to share and evaluate each other’s work. Lab report formats vary slightly among scientific disciplines, but all are based on the IMRAD outline: introduction, materials and methods, results, and discussion. The purpose of each section dictates what information to include, regardless of the specialty being written for. Helpful Tip: It is usually easiest to write the methods and results sections first, followed by the discussion and introduction. Title and abstract (if required) should be written last. IMRAD format: Section Purpose Content and Characteristics Title  Describes the content of the report  Allows scientists to locate research of interest when searching databases  Clear, specific, and accurate  Loaded with keywords drawn from the body of the report Abstract  Summarizes the report  Helps researchers decide whether to read the entire paper  One paragraph (200-250 words)  2-3 sentences for each section, summarizing key data and ideas  A complete synopsis, not a teaser (results and discussion must be included) Introduction  Gives background information needed to understand the current research, tracing the development of existing knowledge  Places the new experiments within the context of the field  Identifies gaps in existing knowledge and shows how the present research will fill them  States the specific objectives of the work  Reviews relevant literature, including properly formatted citations  Explains why the study was conducted, and what question it was designed to answer  Briefly describes approach to the problem  Outlines hypothesis(es) to be tested, and predicted results  Written in a mixture of present tense (for generally accepted truths) and past tense (when referencing specific research Lab Reports - IMRAD © The Writing Centre, Saint Mary’s University, 2017 This handout is for personal use only. Reproduction prohibited without permission. IMRAD format continued: Section Purpose Content and Characteristics Materials and Methods  Explains how the experiments were conducted  Provides enough detail that another scientist could repeat the experiment  Gives readers the information they need to evaluate the validity of results and conclusions  Written in paragraph format  Materials are mentioned while describing methods, never listed separately  Describes the purpose of each procedure, as well as necessary steps  Omits details that are common knowledge or would not impact the results  Written in past tense (recounts what was done, rather than giving instructions) Results  Describes the outcomes of the experiments  Draws attention to key findings and relationships  Allows readers to form their own conclusions based on the data  Straightforward reporting of observations and calculations  Does not include commentary or interpretation  Detailed data is presented in tables and figures, which are referenced in the text  Written portion should summarize and emphasize, not repeat details shown in the visuals  Written in past tense Discussion  Interprets the results and explains their significance  Places the new data in the context of the field  Identifies limitations of the study and suggests next steps  References key data, describing its implications  Identifies any errors made during the experiment and their impacts  Discusses any shortcomings of the protocols or experimental designs  Draws conclusions  Identifies questions that could not be answered  Cites relevant literature  Written in past, present, and future tense, as appropriate References  Provides full bibliographic information, directing the reader to relevant literature  Includes only literature that is cited in the text  Follows a consistent scientific citation style, such as APA

text:
Hello everyone 🙏

This channel is designed to help you explore the world of freelancing and the various opportunities it offers.

Whether you’re looking to work remotely, earn extra income, build your portfolio, or gain real-world experience, freelancing can be a fantastic avenue for you. 

To get started, we invite you to read this report on the state of software engineering in Algeria. It contains valuable information, including a section for remote working: https://state-of-algeria.dev/ You can also start exploring popular freelancing platforms such as Fiverr and Upwork.

Anything you would like to say Do you think bureaucrats in various sectors in Algeria will Foster innovation and spend big money, or you think a lot... That's an important question, with some colleagues, we are trying to initiate collaborations with some institutions and companies, and we've realized a few key points: - Bureaucrats or decision-makers need to show results to keep their positions. - They are eager to showcase results, even if they don't fully understand them. - To initiate collaboration, don't target the bureaucrats or decision-makers directly, as they may not fully understand the initiative and reject it. Instead, focus on collaborating with a younger, more flexible person within the institution. This person will be instrumental in pushing things forward. - Once the bureaucrate sees the potential for results, he will likely follow and support the initiative as he recognize its value in showcasing results

Anything you would like to say What would your advice be for the ones that wanna continue studying abroad for a phd ?(after the 5 years) - Choose during the fifth year, an end-of-study project related to scientific research. - Publish research papers based on your work to showcase your research skills and ability to write research papers. - Apply to a PhD program that aligns with the topic of your end-of-study project, as this will increase your chances compared to other applicants. - Prepare a strong application letter that highlights your skills, motivation, and your previous experience with that topic.

any thing u see it very necessary to learn (soft or hard skills ) out of the program of ENSIA ? Let's talk about soft skills, as they are often underrated. Currently, you are mostly judged based on your grades and hard skills, but after graduation, soft skills will play a more significant role in your career. Inyour professional life, you will have to interact and deal with many people, including recruiters, colleagues, superiors, supervisors, etc. There are many soft skills that can help you effectively deal with people, including effective communication, collaboration, teamwork, adaptability, flexibility, conflict resolution, leadership, etc. For example, something | have noticed is that people are more likely to work and collaborate with individuals who demonstrates flexibility and adaptability in their interactions compared to individuals who are rigid in their interactions, even if they have more hard skills. In general, such flexible individuals tend to get more career opportunities.

Any question, idea, suggestion, etc. pursue a Phd ? No seriously, there are generally two paths after getting a PhD: academia or industry. In academia, a PhD allows you to conduct in-dept research, contribute to your field, and potentially become a researcher and/or professor. Inindustry, it can lead to specialized roles, advanced research positions, and opportunities to work on cutting-edge projects as a consultant. Both paths offer unique challenges and rewards, depending on your career goals and interests.

text:
Hi everyone, 

The "ENSIA Impact" server has two main goals:

1. Explore Career Opportunities:
We’re here to share information and guide you through various career paths after your studies, such as:

   - Working as an engineer in a company
   - Starting your own company or startup
   - Freelancing
   - Consulting
   - Engaging in scientific research or even pursuing a PhD

2. Apply Your Knowledge in practice: 
Our second goal is to help you put the knowledge you acquire and projects you work on during your studies into practice. The aim is to create real-world impact, add value, and even turn your projects into profitable ventures.

Hopefully we will do our best so you can benefit from it Inchallah 🙏

text:
Good morning everyone,

This channel is dedicated to providing all the information related to the school's incubator, led by Dr. Brahimi.

The incubator's mission is to support and guide students in transforming their innovative ideas into viable startups, fostering a culture of entrepreneurship and innovation within the academic environment.

To begin, we encourage you to read Decree 1275, issued by the Ministry of Higher Education in 2022, which introduces the initiative:  
"A Diploma - A Startup / A Diploma - A Patent."

You will find in it the main purpose and advantages of this iniative

للطلبة املنخرطين في مشروع شه ادة-مؤسسة ناشئة/شهادة–براءة اختراع بين الطلبة أعضاء فريق العمل واألساتذة امل شرفين على العمل هذا من جهة طرف املخترعين أما الجامعة فتعتبر املالك املعنوي ل براءة االختراع كونه يسجل باسمها(يمكن ملدير الجامعة أن يتنازل عن الحق التج اري لبراءة االختراع لصالح املخترعين). أما في حالة إشراك طرف خارجي في براءة االختراع كANVREDETفيفضل إرفاق طلب تسجيل براءة االختراع باتفاقية تحدد الحقوق والواجبات. براءات االختراع :ثالثا :ملكية براءة االختراع تفعيل االتفاقية املمضاة بين وزارة التعليم العالي والبحث العلمي ووزارة اقتصاد املعرفة واملؤسسات الن اشئة واملؤسسات املصغرة وذلك لحث الجهات املمولة( صندوق تمويل املؤسسات الناشئةASFوالوكالة الوطنية لترقية وتطوير املقاوالتيةANADE ...الخ)على االلتزام بأخذ مشاريع الطلبة كأولوية) . 2 1 3 4 آليات التمويل 5 تسهيل إجراءات الحصول على الدعم املالي خاصة فيما يتعلق بحصول الطلبة املنخرطين في مشروع شهادة–مؤسسة ناشئة على وسم"البل"مشروع مبتكر و"البل مؤسسة ناشئة"؛ . يتكفل مسرع األعمال( Algérien Venture , DGRSDT et A NVREDET ) و الجهات املمولة بتكوين األساتذة املدربين في املرافقة في إجراءات الحصول على الدعم املالي( صندوق تمويل املؤسسات الناشئةASF )؛ توجيه املشاريع التي يقل فيها معدل االبتكار إلى الحد املطلوب إلى وكالة دعم وتطوير املقاوالتيةANADE فتح املجال لكل آليات التمويل األخرى املتاحة لتمويل مشاريع الطلبة املنخرطين في مشروع شهادة– مؤ سسة ناشئة. . مالحظة عامة تأخذ اللجنة الوطنية بعين االعتبار عدد املؤسسات ال ناشة وبراءات text:
Hello everyone,

Our school has been invited to select 10 students to participate in the upcoming WaterTech bootcamp.

The goal is to propose innovative ideas for water treatment and receive training to develop them into sustainable projects and startups.

If you're interested, please read the details and start forming your teams (10 members, 2nd and 3rd-year students only). We will send you the team submission form soon.

text:
Non-exhaustive list of projects proposed by the library of our school, in case you would like to work on them in courses where you have the freedom to select your project's topic. Another example of project is a library management system. 

These kinds of projects allow you to practice with real projects while benefiting the school and the other students from your solutions.
raw_content:
Non-exhaustive list of projects proposed by the library of our school, in case you would like to work on them in courses where you have the freedom to select your project's topic. Another example of project is a library management system. 

These kinds of projects allow you to practice with real projects while benefiting the school and the other students from your solutions.

[Contains image]

[Extracted image text]
Création d'un site web pour la bibliotheque. Créer un systeme de recommandation de livres en fonction des préférences des lecteurs et de leur historique de prét. Développer un programme pour numériser des livres et créer une bibliotheque numérique. Sécurité et suivi des livres via RFID. Développer une application qui génére automatiquement des résumés de livres en utilisant des techniques de traitement du langage naturel. Créer une plateforme permettant a la bibliotheque de planifier des événements. Créer un catalogue numérique interactif permettant aux utilisateurs de rechercher des livres, soit de maniére simple par titre, auteur, mot-clé, soit avec une recherche avancée (ISBN, éditeur, etc.). Création d'un dépot institutionnel (D-space).

Développer une application qui génére automatiquement des résumés de livres en utilisant des techniques de traitement du langage naturel. Créer une plateforme permettant a la bibliotheque de planifier des événements. Créer un catalogue numérique interactif permettant aux utilisateurs de rechercher des livres, soit de maniére simple par titre, auteur, mot-clé, soit avec une recherche avancée (ISBN, éditeur, etc.). Création d'un dépot institutionnel (D-space).

Consulting could be another future career path for AI and tech students. In this channel, we will share useful information about what consulting involves and provide resources to help you explore this path.

To get started, we invite you to read this webpage from Boardroom Advisors, which explains how AI consultants help businesses implement AI solutions to enhance efficiency and growth. https://boardroomadvisors.co/what-is-an-ai-consultant/

ــة الـدّيـمـقــراطـيّـة الـشﹽـعـبـيﹽـة وحـكـومــة الـجـمـهـوريــة اﻹسﻼميّة اﳌوريتانية. إنّ حكومة اﳉمهوريّة اﳉزائريّة الدّيمقراطيّة الشّ عبيّة من جهة، وحكومة اﳉمهورية اﻹسﻼمية اﳌوريتانية من جهة أخرى، اﳌشار إليهما فيما يأتي“بـالـطـرفﲔ”، اﳉريدة الرسميّة للجمهوريّ ة اﳉزائريّ ة / العدد ٨7١٢ جمادى اﻷوﱃ عام ٥4 4١ هـ ٥ ديسمبر سنة ٣٢٠٢ م6 ج– لقب واسم وصفة وجنسية ومسكن أو إقامة أطراف الدعوى والعنوان اﻻجتماعي بالنسبة لﻸشخاص اﳌعنوية، د– لقب واسم وعنوان ﳑثﲇ اﻷطراف، عند اﻻقتضاء، هـ– موضوع الطلب والوثائق اﳌرفقة، و– أي بيانات أخرى ضرورية ﻹنجاز اﻹجراءات اﳌطلوبة. ٣– ﰲ حالة تبليغ اﻷحكام القضائية، يشار ﰲ الطلب إﱃ آجال وطرق الطعن وفقا لتشريع البلد الذي صدر فيه اﳊكم. اﳌادة ٩ مصاريف التعاون القضائي ﻻ يترتب عن تنفيذ التعاون القضائي تسديد أي مصاريف باستثناء أتعاب اﳋبراء. اﳌادة ٠١ إثبات تبليغ الوثائق ١– يـثـبـت تـبـلـيـغ الـوثـائــق القـضـائـيـة وغـيـر الـقضـائيـة إمّا بواسطة وصل مؤرّخ وموقّ ع من اﳌرسل إليه أو بواسطة شهادة من السلطة اﳌطلوبة تثبت فعل وطريقة وتاريخ التسليم. ٢– إذا تعذر التسليم، يحاط الطرف الطالب علما بذلك. اﳌادة ١١ اﻹنابات القضائية تتضمن اﻹنابات القضائية البيانات اﻵتية : أ– السلطة القضائية الطالبة، ب– السلـطـة الـقضائـيـة اﳌطـلـوب مـنـهـا الـتـنـفـيـذ، عـنـد اﻻقتضاء، ج– لقب واسم وعنوان وصفة اﻷطراف والشهود، د– موضوع الطلب واﻹجراءات اﳌطلوب تنفيذها، هـ– اﻷسئلة التي يجب طرحها عﲆ الشهود، عند اﻻقتضاء، و– أي بيانات أخرى ﻹنجاز اﻹجراءات اﳌطلوبة. اﳌادة ٢١ تنفيذ اﻹنابات القضائية ١– تنـفــذ اﻹنـابـات القـضـائيـة فـي إقـلـيم أحـد الطـرفﲔ عن طريق السلطة القضائية حسب اﻹجراءات اﳌتبعة ﰲ كل منهما. ٢– إن الشهادة اﳌثبتة لعدم كفاية اﳌوارد اﳌالية تسلّم إﱃ الشخص الطالب من طرف السلطات اﳌختصة لبلده إذا ما كان يقيم أو يسكن ﰲ إقليم أحد الطرفﲔ وتسـلـّم هذه الشهـادة مـن طـرف اﳌمـثـلـيـات الـدبـلـومـاسيـة أو الـقـنصلـيـة لـبـلـده اﳌـخـتـصة إقـلـيـمـيا إذا مـا كــان الـشـخـص اﳌـعنـي يـقـيم أو يسكن ﰲ بلد ثالث. اﳌادة ٥ اﻹعفاء من التصديق تعفى الوثائق اﳌرسلة تطبيقا ﻷحكام هذه اﻻتفاقية من أي شكل من أشكال التصديق. ويــجب أن ﲤهــر بــالــتــوقــيــع واﳋتــم الــرسمي لــلسلــطــة اﳌختصة بإصدارها. الفصل الثاني التعاون القضائي اﳌادة ٦ نطاق التعاون القضائي يشمل التعاون القضائي تبليغ وإرسال الوثائق القضائية وغير القضائية، وتنفيذ إجراءات قضائية كسماع الشهود أو اﻷطراف، أو إجراء خبرة أو اﳊصول عﲆ اﻷدلة وتبادل وثائق اﳊالة اﳌدنية، وكذا كل إجراء آخر ﰲ إطار ﲢقيق قضائي بناء عﲆ طلب أحد الطرفﲔ. اﳌادة 7 رفض التعاون القضائي يرفض التعاون القضائي إذا اعتبر الطرف اﳌطلوب منه التعاون أن هذا التعاون من شأنه اﳌساس بسيادته أو أمنه أو نظامه العام. اﳌادة ٨ إرسال طلبات التعاون القضائي ١– يـرسل طـلب الـتـعـاون مـبـاشرة مـن اﳉهـة الـقضائـيـة اﳌختصة إﱃ نظيرتها ﰲ بلد الطرف اﻵخر الذي يقيم اﳌعني ﰲ دائرة اختصاصها. إذا كانت اﳉهة القضائية اﳌقدم إليها الطلب غير مختصة فـتـوجـه هـذا اﻷخـيـر مـن تـلـقـاء نـفسهـا إﱃ اﳉهـة الـقضائـيـة اﳌختصة، وتعلم بذلك فورا اﳉهة الطالبة. ٢– يحتوي طلب التعاون القضائي عﲆ البيانات اﻵتية : أ– السلطة القضائية الطالبة، ب– السلـطـة الـقضائـيـة اﳌطـلـوب مـنـهـا الـتـعـاون، عـنـد اﻻقتضاء، اﳉريدة الرسميّة للجمهوريّ ة اﳉزائريّ ة / العدد ٨7١٢ جمادى اﻷوﱃ عام ٥4 4١ هـ ٥ ديسمبر سنة ٣٢٠٢ م7 ٢– تقوم السلطة اﳌطلوب منها التنفيذ، بطلب صريح من السلطة الطالبة، ﲟا يأتي : أ– تنفيذ اﻹنابة القضائية وفق شكل خاص إذا كان ذلك غير مخالف لتشريع بلدها، ب– إعﻼم السلطة الطالبة ﰲ الوقت اﳌناسب بتاريخ ومكان تنفيذ اﻹنابة القضائية حتى يتسنى لﻸطراف اﳌعنية اﳊضور طبقا لتشريع البلد اﳌطلوب منه التنفيذ. ٣– ﰲ حالة عدم إنجاز الطلب، ترد الوثائق اﳌرفقة به إﱃ الطرف الطالب، ويجب إخطاره عن أسباب عدم إنجاز الطلب أو رفضه. اﳌادة ٣١ مثول الشهود واﳋبراء ١– إذا كان اﳌثول الشخصي لشاهد أو خبير أمام السلطات القضائية للطرف الطالب ضروريا، فإنّ السلطة القضائية ﰲ البلد التي يوجد مقر إقامته أو مسكنه بها تقوم بدعوته إﱃ الرد عﲆ اﻻستدعاءات اﳌوجهة إليه. ٢– ﰲ هذه اﳊالة، ﲤنح مصاريف السفر وتعويضات اﻹقامة للشاهد أو اﳋبير من محل إقامته حسب التعريفات والتنظيمات النافذة ﰲ الدولة التي سوف يتم فيها السماع، وتشمل مصاريف السفر تذكرة الطائرة ذهابا وإيابا إﱃ اﳌطار اﻷقرب من مقر اﳉهة القضائية التي يمثل أمامها الشاهد أو اﳋبير. وبناء عﲆ طلب هذا اﻷخير تضمن السلطات القـنـصـلـيـة للـطـرف الـطـالـب تـذكـرة الـسـفـر أو تـسـبـيقـا عـن اﳌصاريف اﳌرتبطة به. ٣– وﰲ حالة عدم اﳌثول، ﻻ تتخذ السلطة اﳌطلوب منها التنفيذ ضد اﻷشخاص اﳌتخلفﲔ أي إجراء ردعي. 4 – ﻻ يجوز متابعة أو حبس الشاهد أو اﳋبير اﳌاثل من أجل أفعال أو تنفيذا ﻷحكام سابقة ﳌغادرته إقليم الطرف اﳌطلوب منه التعاون. ٥– تزول هذه اﳊصانة إذا لم يغادر الشاهد أو اﳋبير الذي كانت له اﳊرية ﰲ ذلك، إقليم الطرف الطالب خﻼل خمسة عـشـر )٥1 ( يـومــا مـن تـبـلـيغــه أنّ حـضـوره لـم يـعـد ضرورياﹰ أو عاد إليه باختياره بعد أن غادره. اﳌادة 4١ تسليم الوثائق القضائية وغير القضائية وتنفيذ اﻹنابات القضائية من قبل اﳌمثليات الدبلوماسية أو القنصلية يمكن كل طرف تسليم الوثائق القضائية وغير القضائية إﱃ مواطنيه أو القيام بسماعهم مباشرة عن طريق ﳑثلياته الدبلوماسية أو القنصلية طبقا لتشريع كل من الطرفﲔ. الفصل الثالث اﻻعتراف وتنفيذ اﻷحكام أو القرارات القضائية والعقود الرسمية والقرارات التحكيمية اﳌادة ٥١ الشروط اﳌطلوبة لﻼعتراف باﻷحكام أو القرارات القضائية وتنفيذها إنّ اﻷحكام أو القرارات الصادرة عن اﳉهات القضائية للطرفﲔ ﰲ اﳌواد اﳌدنية والتجارية ﲟا فيها تلك اﳌتعلقة باﳊقوق اﳌدنية اﶈكوم بها من قبل اﳉهات القضائية اﳉزائية يعترف بها وتنفذ وفقا للشروط اﻵتية : أ– أن يصدر اﳊكم أو القرار من جهة قضائية مختصة، ب– أن يثبت بأنه قد ﰎ استدعاء اﻷطراف بصفة صحيحة أو ﰎ ﲤثيلهم أو ﰎ التصريح بغيابهم وفق قانون الطرف الذي أصدر اﳊكم أو القرار، ج– أن يصبح اﳊكم أو القرار نهائيا طبقا لقانون الطرف الذي صدر فيه، د– أن يكون اﳊكم أو القرار غير مخالف للنظام العام للطرف اﳌطلوب فيه التنفيذ. اﳌادة ٦١ إجراءات اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ ١– تخضع إجراءات اﻻعتراف وتنفيذ اﻷحكام أو القرارات القضائية للتشريع الساري اﳌفعول لدى الطرف اﳌطلوب فيه اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ. ٢– يجب أن يقدم طلب اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ مباشرة من الشخص اﳌعني إﱃ السلطة القضائية اﳌختصة للطرف اﳌطـلـوب مـنـه تـنـفـيـذ اﳊكـم أو الـقـرار واﻻعـتـراف بـه فـوق إقليمه. اﳌادة 7١ الوثائق اﳌرفقة بطلب اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ يجب عﲆ الشخص اﳌعني الذي يطلب اﻻعتراف وتنفيذ اﳊكم أو القرار أن يقدم ما يأتي : أ– صورة رسمية للحكم أو القرار تتوفر فيها الشروط الﻼزمة ﻹثبات صحتها، ب– شهـــادة تـــثــبت أن اﳊكــم أو الــقــرار نــهــائي وفــقــا لتشريع البلد الذي صدر فيه، ج– أصل محضر تبليغ اﳊكم أو القرار أو أي وثيقة أخرى ﲢل محل محضر التبليغ، اﳉريدة الرسميّة للجمهوريّ ة اﳉزائريّ ة / العدد ٨7١٢ جمادى اﻷوﱃ عام ٥4 4١ هـ ٥ ديسمبر سنة ٣٢٠٢ م8 د– نسخة رسمية من التكليف باﳊضور اﳌوجه إﱃ اﳋصم الذي تخلف عن اﳊضور ﰲ حالة صدور حكم غيابي وذلك عندما ﻻ يتبيّـن من اﳊكم أو القرار أن تبليغ التكليف باﳊضور كان صحيحا. اﳌادة ٨١ اﻻعتراف بالعقود الرسمية وتنفيذها ١– يـعـلـن عـن نـفـاذ الـعـقـود الـرسـمـيـة، ﻻ سيـما منها العقود التوثيقية ﰲ إقليم أحد الطرفﲔ من طرف السلطة اﳌختصة طبقا لقانون الطرف الذي يتم لديه التنفيذ. ٢– تقوم السلطة اﳌختصة بالتحقق فقط من أن العقود تستوﰲ الشروط اﳌطلوبة لرسمية السند وأن تتوفر فيه صفة السند التنفيذي وقابليته للتنفيذ وفقا لتشريع البلد الـذي حـرّ رت فـيـه، والـتـأكـد مـن عـدم مخالفتها للنظام العام للطرف اﳌطلوب فيه اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ. اﳌادة ٩١ اﻻعتراف بالقرارات التحكيمية وتنفيذها إنّ القرارات التحكيمية الصادرة ﰲ إقليم الطرفﲔ يتم اﻻعتراف بها وتنفيذها وفقا ﻷحكام اﻻتفاقية التي صادق عليها مؤﲤر اﻷﱈ اﳌتحدة ﰲ نيويورك بتاريخ ٠1 يونيو سنة 8٥9 1. اﳌادة ٠٢ تبادل اﳌعلومات والوثائق يتعهد الطرفان، وبطلب أحد منهما، بتبادل اﳌعلومات والوثائق ﰲ اﳌجال التشريعي واﻻجتهاد القضائي. الفصل الرابع أحكام ختامية اﳌادة ١٢ التصديق يصادق عﲆ هذه اﻻتفاقية طبقا للقواعد الدستورية لكل طرف. اﳌادة ٢٢ دخول اﻻتفاقية حيز اﻻجتماعي بالنسبة لﻸشخاص اﳌعنوية، د– لقب واسم وعنوان ﳑثﲇ اﻷطراف، عند اﻻقتضاء، هـ– موضوع الطلب والوثائق اﳌرفقة، و– أي بيانات أخرى ضرورية ﻹنجاز اﻹجراءات اﳌطلوبة. ٣– ﰲ حالة تبليغ اﻷحكام القضائية، يشار ﰲ الطلب إﱃ آجال وطرق الطعن وفقا لتشريع البلد الذي صدر فيه اﳊكم. اﳌادة ٩ مصاريف التعاون القضائي ﻻ يترتب عن تنفيذ التعاون القضائي تسديد أي مصاريف باستثناء أتعاب اﳋبراء. اﳌادة ٠١ إثبات تبليغ الوثائق ١– يـثـبـت تـبـلـيـغ الـوثـائــق القـضـائـيـة وغـيـر الـقضـائيـة إمّا بواسطة وصل مؤرّخ وموقّ ع من اﳌرسل إليه أو بواسطة شهادة من السلطة اﳌطلوبة تثبت فعل وطريقة وتاريخ التسليم. ٢– إذا تعذر التسليم، يحاط الطرف الطالب علما بذلك. اﳌادة ١١ اﻹنابات القضائية تتضمن اﻹنابات القضائية البيانات اﻵتية : أ– السلطة القضائية الطالبة، ب– السلـطـة الـقضائـيـة اﳌطـلـوب مـنـهـا الـتـنـفـيـذ، عـنـد اﻻقتضاء، ج– لقب واسم وعنوان وصفة اﻷطراف والشهود، د– موضوع الطلب واﻹجراءات اﳌطلوب تنفيذها، هـ– اﻷسئلة التي يجب طرحها عﲆ الشهود، عند اﻻقتضاء، و– أي بيانات أخرى ﻹنجاز اﻹجراءات اﳌطلوبة. اﳌادة ٢١ تنفيذ اﻹنابات القضائية ١– تنـفــذ اﻹنـابـات القـضـائيـة فـي إقـلـيم أحـد الطـرفﲔ عن طريق السلطة القضائية حسب اﻹجراءات اﳌتبعة ﰲ كل منهما. ٢– إن الشهادة اﳌثبتة لعدم كفاية اﳌوارد اﳌالية تسلّم إﱃ الشخص الطالب من طرف السلطات اﳌختصة لبلده إذا ما كان يقيم أو يسكن ﰲ إقليم أحد الطرفﲔ وتسـلـّم هذه الشهـادة مـن طـرف اﳌمـثـلـيـات الـدبـلـومـاسيـة أو الـقـنصلـيـة لـبـلـده اﳌـخـتـصة إقـلـيـمـيا إذا مـا كــان الـشـخـص اﳌـعنـي يـقـيم أو يسكن ﰲ بلد ثالث. اﳌادة ٥ اﻹعفاء من التصديق تعفى الوثائق اﳌرسلة تطبيقا ﻷحكام هذه اﻻتفاقية من أي شكل من أشكال التصديق. ويــجب أن ﲤهــر بــالــتــوقــيــع واﳋتــم الــرسمي لــلسلــطــة اﳌختصة بإصدارها. الفصل الثاني التعاون القضائي اﳌادة ٦ نطاق التعاون القضائي يشمل التعاون القضائي تبليغ وإرسال الوثائق القضائية وغير القضائية، وتنفيذ إجراءات قضائية كسماع الشهود أو اﻷطراف، أو إجراء خبرة أو اﳊصول عﲆ اﻷدلة وتبادل وثائق اﳊالة اﳌدنية، وكذا كل إجراء آخر ﰲ إطار ﲢقيق قضائي بناء عﲆ طلب أحد الطرفﲔ. اﳌادة 7 رفض التعاون القضائي يرفض التعاون القضائي إذا اعتبر الطرف اﳌطلوب منه التعاون أن هذا التعاون من شأنه اﳌساس بسيادته أو أمنه أو نظامه العام. اﳌادة ٨ إرسال طلبات التعاون القضائي ١– يـرسل طـلب الـتـعـاون مـبـاشرة مـن اﳉهـة الـقضائـيـة اﳌختصة إﱃ نظيرتها ﰲ بلد الطرف اﻵخر الذي يقيم اﳌعني ﰲ دائرة اختصاصها. إذا كانت اﳉهة القضائية اﳌقدم إليها الطلب غير مختصة فـتـوجـه هـذا اﻷخـيـر مـن تـلـقـاء نـفسهـا إﱃ اﳉهـة الـقضائـيـة اﳌختصة، وتعلم بذلك فورا اﳉهة الطالبة. ٢– يحتوي طلب التعاون القضائي عﲆ البيانات اﻵتية : أ– السلطة القضائية الطالبة، ب– السلـطـة الـقضائـيـة اﳌطـلـوب مـنـهـا الـتـعـاون، عـنـد اﻻقتضاء، اﳉريدة الرسميّة للجمهوريّ ة اﳉزائريّ ة / العدد ٨7١٢ جمادى اﻷوﱃ عام ٥4 4١ هـ ٥ ديسمبر سنة ٣٢٠٢ م7 ٢– تقوم السلطة اﳌطلوب منها التنفيذ، بطلب صريح من السلطة الطالبة، ﲟا يأتي : أ– تنفيذ اﻹنابة القضائية وفق شكل خاص إذا كان ذلك غير مخالف لتشريع بلدها، ب– إعﻼم السلطة الطالبة ﰲ الوقت اﳌناسب بتاريخ ومكان تنفيذ اﻹنابة القضائية حتى يتسنى لﻸطراف اﳌعنية اﳊضور طبقا لتشريع البلد اﳌطلوب منه التنفيذ. ٣– ﰲ حالة عدم إنجاز الطلب، ترد الوثائق اﳌرفقة به إﱃ الطرف الطالب، ويجب إخطاره عن أسباب عدم إنجاز الطلب أو رفضه. اﳌادة ٣١ مثول الشهود واﳋبراء ١– إذا كان اﳌثول الشخصي لشاهد أو خبير أمام السلطات القضائية للطرف الطالب ضروريا، فإنّ السلطة القضائية ﰲ البلد التي يوجد مقر إقامته أو مسكنه بها تقوم بدعوته إﱃ الرد عﲆ اﻻستدعاءات اﳌوجهة إليه. ٢– ﰲ هذه اﳊالة، ﲤنح مصاريف السفر وتعويضات اﻹقامة للشاهد أو اﳋبير من محل إقامته حسب التعريفات والتنظيمات النافذة ﰲ الدولة التي سوف يتم فيها السماع، وتشمل مصاريف السفر تذكرة الطائرة ذهابا وإيابا إﱃ اﳌطار اﻷقرب من مقر اﳉهة القضائية التي يمثل أمامها الشاهد أو اﳋبير. وبناء عﲆ طلب هذا اﻷخير تضمن السلطات القـنـصـلـيـة للـطـرف الـطـالـب تـذكـرة الـسـفـر أو تـسـبـيقـا عـن اﳌصاريف اﳌرتبطة به. ٣– وﰲ حالة عدم اﳌثول، ﻻ تتخذ السلطة اﳌطلوب منها التنفيذ ضد اﻷشخاص اﳌتخلفﲔ أي إجراء ردعي. 4 – ﻻ يجوز متابعة أو حبس الشاهد أو اﳋبير اﳌاثل من أجل أفعال أو تنفيذا ﻷحكام سابقة ﳌغادرته إقليم الطرف اﳌطلوب منه التعاون. ٥– تزول هذه اﳊصانة إذا لم يغادر الشاهد أو اﳋبير الذي كانت له اﳊرية ﰲ ذلك، إقليم الطرف الطالب خﻼل خمسة عـشـر )٥1 ( يـومــا مـن تـبـلـيغــه أنّ حـضـوره لـم يـعـد ضرورياﹰ أو عاد إليه باختياره بعد أن غادره. اﳌادة 4١ تسليم الوثائق القضائية وغير القضائية وتنفيذ اﻹنابات القضائية من قبل اﳌمثليات الدبلوماسية أو القنصلية يمكن كل طرف تسليم الوثائق القضائية وغير القضائية إﱃ مواطنيه أو القيام بسماعهم مباشرة عن طريق ﳑثلياته الدبلوماسية أو القنصلية طبقا لتشريع كل من الطرفﲔ. الفصل الثالث اﻻعتراف وتنفيذ اﻷحكام أو القرارات القضائية والعقود الرسمية والقرارات التحكيمية اﳌادة ٥١ الشروط اﳌطلوبة لﻼعتراف باﻷحكام أو القرارات القضائية وتنفيذها إنّ اﻷحكام أو القرارات الصادرة عن اﳉهات القضائية للطرفﲔ ﰲ اﳌواد اﳌدنية والتجارية ﲟا فيها تلك اﳌتعلقة باﳊقوق اﳌدنية اﶈكوم بها من قبل اﳉهات القضائية اﳉزائية يعترف بها وتنفذ وفقا للشروط اﻵتية : أ– أن يصدر اﳊكم أو القرار من جهة قضائية مختصة، ب– أن يثبت بأنه قد ﰎ استدعاء اﻷطراف بصفة صحيحة أو ﰎ ﲤثيلهم أو ﰎ التصريح بغيابهم وفق قانون الطرف الذي أصدر اﳊكم أو القرار، ج– أن يصبح اﳊكم أو القرار نهائيا طبقا لقانون الطرف الذي صدر فيه، د– أن يكون اﳊكم أو القرار غير مخالف للنظام العام للطرف اﳌطلوب فيه التنفيذ. اﳌادة ٦١ إجراءات اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ ١– تخضع إجراءات اﻻعتراف وتنفيذ اﻷحكام أو القرارات القضائية للتشريع الساري اﳌفعول لدى الطرف اﳌطلوب فيه اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ. ٢– يجب أن يقدم طلب اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ مباشرة من الشخص اﳌعني إﱃ السلطة القضائية اﳌختصة للطرف اﳌطـلـوب مـنـه تـنـفـيـذ اﳊكـم أو الـقـرار واﻻعـتـراف بـه فـوق إقليمه. اﳌادة 7١ الوثائق اﳌرفقة بطلب اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ يجب عﲆ الشخص اﳌعني الذي يطلب اﻻعتراف وتنفيذ اﳊكم أو القرار أن يقدم ما يأتي : أ– صورة رسمية للحكم أو القرار تتوفر فيها الشروط الﻼزمة ﻹثبات صحتها، ب– شهـــادة تـــثــبت أن اﳊكــم أو الــقــرار نــهــائي وفــقــا لتشريع البلد الذي صدر فيه، ج– أصل محضر تبليغ اﳊكم أو القرار أو أي وثيقة أخرى ﲢل محل محضر التبليغ، اﳉريدة الرسميّة للجمهوريّ ة اﳉزائريّ ة / العدد ٨7١٢ جمادى اﻷوﱃ عام ٥4 4١ هـ ٥ ديسمبر سنة ٣٢٠٢ م8 د– نسخة رسمية من التكليف باﳊضور اﳌوجه إﱃ اﳋصم الذي تخلف عن اﳊضور ﰲ حالة صدور حكم غيابي وذلك عندما ﻻ يتبيّـن من اﳊكم أو القرار أن تبليغ التكليف باﳊضور كان صحيحا. اﳌادة ٨١ اﻻعتراف بالعقود الرسمية وتنفيذها ١– يـعـلـن عـن نـفـاذ الـعـقـود الـرسـمـيـة، ﻻ سيـما منها العقود التوثيقية ﰲ إقليم أحد الطرفﲔ من طرف السلطة اﳌختصة طبقا لقانون الطرف الذي يتم لديه التنفيذ. ٢– تقوم السلطة اﳌختصة بالتحقق فقط من أن العقود تستوﰲ الشروط اﳌطلوبة لرسمية السند وأن تتوفر فيه صفة السند التنفيذي وقابليته للتنفيذ وفقا لتشريع البلد الـذي حـرّ رت فـيـه، والـتـأكـد مـن عـدم مخالفتها للنظام العام للطرف اﳌطلوب فيه اﻻعتراف والتنفيذ. اﳌادة ٩١ اﻻعتراف بالقرارات التحكيمية وتنفيذها إنّ القرارات التحكيمية الصادرة ﰲ إقليم الطرفﲔ يتم اﻻعتراف بها وتنفيذها وفقا ﻷحكام اﻻتفاقية التي صادق عليها مؤﲤر اﻷﱈ اﳌتحدة ﰲ نيويورك بتاريخ ٠1 يونيو سنة 8٥9 1. اﳌادة ٠٢ تبادل اﳌعلومات والوثائق يتعهد الطرفان، وبطلب text:
Salam all, this is a very interesting Algerian Discord server for anyone interested in startups and entrepreneurship.

I really like the topics, ideas, and resources being discussed and shared there.

The server also includes many experienced individuals who provide valuable advice regarding the Algerian context.

One of these experts is Mr. Abdelmalek CHETTA, co-founder of the Itihad group and member of the GAAN board (Groupement Algérien des Acteurs du Numérique) https://discord.gg/nfzMGfCP

text:
[Third and Fourth-year students] 

Djezzy has launched the "Rise Up" program, a coaching program to support students with innovative project ideas.

Mentors from Djezzy will guide the program, covering various fields: law, finance, project management, data protection, cybersecurity, and personal development.

The training sessions will take place in person, likely during the winter break (December).

The school is requested to select 10 projects for the program. For each project, only one team member will participate in the training.

Each team is asked to designate one representative to complete the form below: https://forms.gle/MHa66PnnEmm4bwpD7 Deadline: Saturday at midnight

text: Advice to find internships in a company, even if it's not a partner:
raw_content: Advice to find internships in a company, even if it's not a partner:

[Contains image]

[Extracted image text]
* Showcase your skills: Some of you have strong abilities, but recruiters won't see them unless you clearly demonstrate it through your CV, GitHub. repository, and motivation letter. Remember, recruiters don't know anything about you and will first rely on these resources before going further. Enhance your CV and GitHub: Some CVs are too brief, and some GitHub repositories are almost empty. Both should be improved to better reflect the significant projects you have worked on. A well-organized portfolio can also be a great way to showcase all your work in one place. Targeted Outreach: When searching internships, sending emails to a company's general “contact” address might not be effective. The person managing that inbox may not be from the IT field and may not forward your email to the right person.
media_text: * Showcase your skills: Some of you have strong abilities, but recruiters won't see them unless you clearly demonstrate it through your CV, GitHub. repository, and motivation letter. Remember, recruiters don't know anything about you and will first rely on these resources before going further. Enhance your CV and GitHub: Some CVs are too brief, and some GitHub repositories are almost empty. Both should be improved to better reflect the significant projects you have worked on. A well-organized portfolio can also be a great way to showcase all your work in one place. Targeted Outreach: When searching internships, sending emails to a company's general “contact” address might not be effective. The person managing that inbox may not be from the IT field and may not forward your email to the right person.

raw_content:
[Contains image]

[Extracted image text]
* Use LinkedIn effectively: LinkedIn is faster and more efficient than visiting companies in person. | recommend searching for the company on LinkedIn, checking the "People" section, and finding someone in a significant role within that company. Send that person a message explaining your motivation along with your CV and GitHub link to show your value. Personalize your motivation letter: Avoid simple or generic messages that could apply to any application. Instead, research the company and write a motivation letter demonstrating your knowledge of their activities and understanding of the internship’s goals. Convince the recruiter: Explain why the company fits your internship goals and how they can benefit from having you as an intern, including the specific skills or insights you can bring. * Communicate effectively: Ensure your communication provides enough detail while remaining concise to respect the recruiter's time. Adapt your application: Even for a one-month internship, explain how it could be a valuable first contact with the company and lead to further opportunities, like end-of-study projects or summer internships.
media_text: * Use LinkedIn effectively: LinkedIn is faster and more efficient than visiting companies in person. | recommend searching for the company on LinkedIn, checking the "People" section, and finding someone in a significant role within that company. Send that person a message explaining your motivation along with your CV and GitHub link to show your value. Personalize your motivation letter: Avoid simple or generic messages that could apply to any application. Instead, research the company and write a motivation letter demonstrating your knowledge of their activities and understanding of the internship’s goals. Convince the recruiter: Explain why the company fits your internship goals and how they can benefit from having you as an intern, including the specific skills or insights you can bring. * Communicate effectively: Ensure your communication provides enough detail while remaining concise to respect the recruiter's time. Adapt your application: Even for a one-month internship, explain how it could be a valuable first contact with the company and lead to further opportunities, like end-of-study projects or summer internships.

text:
Dear all,

I am thrilled to announce the official creation of the Entrepreneurship Development Centre at our school! This initiative is designed to equip you with the skills and knowledge needed to face the challenges of the labor market and become a valuable asset in the workforce.

The Centre’s responsibilities include:

1. Raising awareness about entrepreneurship;


2. Providing training in strategic planning, including how to create your Business Model Canvas (BMC);


3. Offering support before and after graduation to secure project funding through NESDA (The National Entrepreneurship Support & Development Agency).



To better understand our mission, please review the recently published legal decree in the Official Journal.

Dear all,

I am thrilled to announce the official creation of the Entrepreneurship Development Centre at our school! This initiative is designed to equip you with the skills and knowledge needed to face the challenges of the labor market and become a valuable asset in the workforce.

The Centre’s responsibilities include:

1. Raising awareness about entrepreneurship;


2. Providing training in strategic planning, including how to create your Business Model Canvas (BMC);


3. Offering support before and after graduation to secure project funding through NESDA (The National Entrepreneurship Support & Development Agency).



To better understand our mission, please review the recently published legal decree in the Official Journal.

text:
Good evening all, 

Following the recent WaterTech bootcamp (video available here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1E4psHn7AQX_M6KV6f_MoOZZ-ykrdHYja/view ), two additional bootcamps are scheduled:

- HealthTech: November 23–25
- PollutionTech: November 27–29

We are able to select 10 students for each bootcamp: https://forms.gle/2b3qTB85cXC6BoAd6 Application Requirements:
- Open to 2nd and 3rd-year students only (mixed teams are encouraged).
- Students who participated in the WaterTech bootcamp are not eligible.
- Only team leaders should submit the application form on behalf of their team.
- The deadline for applications is Saturday, November 16, at midnight.

Once selected, the list of students will be forwarded to the Bootcamp organizers, who have indicated they will consider assisting with transportation.

[Contains image]

[Extracted image text]
For the internship, | would recommend considering this: 1. Set your short- and long-term career goals. 2. Select the topics and technologies you want to develop your skills in 3. Choose an internship that aligns with your goals. 4. Prioritize companies that offer appropriate supervision 5- Prioritize companies that provide a motivating learning environment 6. Prioritize companies that use the latest and most relevant technologies. 7. Prioritize companies that can open career opportunities for you. 8. Prioritize companies that provide an internship certificate. 9. Prioritize companies that offer paid internships.

text:
Important information to share:
The number of available PhD positions in AI has significantly increased this year in our country. For context, any engineering or master's degree holder in our country can take the PhD entrance exam at any university. Those who succeed in the exam at a given university will have the opportunity to pursue a PhD (Doctorat) and conduct scientific research at that university 🎓

text:
🚀 AI Consulting Platform – Open Idea for Algeria 🇩🇿

Salam all,

We’ve been experiencing an increasing requests from professionals in various fields (healthcare, agriculture, law, finance, and more) who want to integrate AI into their work but need expert guidance.

💡 Idea: A platform that connects these professionals with AI experts and consultants. This could be a great opportunity for AI graduates and professionals to offer their expertise as a paid service while helping different sectors leverage AI solutions.

We are just sharing this idea—we won’t be working on it personally, but we believe it’s a valuable opportunity for anyone interested. If you see potential in it, feel free to take the lead and make it happen! 🚀

text:
I'm receiving many questions about the Pre-Incubation Program , so here are some key answers: Q1: Can students from all study levels participate? ✅ Yes! We prioritize the potential of the idea and its added value over the study level. Q2: Do I need a project idea to participate? ✅ Yes, you must have a project idea and be fully committed to developing it into a real product. Q3: Does my project need to be in an advanced stage? ✅ No, only an idea and commitment are required. However, more advanced projects will be prioritized for their potential to create value sooner. Q4: What does the Pre-Incubation Program include? ✅ You'll get a temporary spot (2 months) inside the incubator to prepare for next year’s Incubation Program .

✅ You’ll have access to workshops, talks, and coaching sessions to strengthen your application. Q5: What is the role of the ENSIA Incubator? ✅ It boosts the startup ecosystem within ENSIA, helping early-stage projects grow into real businesses.

✅ It provides resources like training, events, workshops, prototype tools and workspace to support your journey.
For more details, stay tuned! 🚀

text:
Salam everyone,

This channel provides guidance and support for students working on their final year project (PFE). Your project choice will shape your expertise and future career. Over months of intensive work, you will become an expert in your field.

Why is this important?

Your dissertation isn’t just a graduation requirement; it’s a chance to develop skills, build expertise, and open doors to future opportunities. Your chosen topic will influence your career, and the type of project you select (research, startup, or industry collaboration) can lead to different paths.
raw_content:
Salam everyone,

This channel provides guidance and support for students working on their final year project (PFE). Your project choice will shape your expertise and future career. Over months of intensive work, you will become an expert in your field.

Why is this important?

Your dissertation isn’t just a graduation requirement; it’s a chance to develop skills, build expertise, and open doors to future opportunities. Your chosen topic will influence your career, and the type of project you select (research, startup, or industry collaboration) can lead to different paths.

text:
[First Guide: Choosing the Right AI Dissertation Topic]

We've compiled 10 criteria to help you choose the right AI dissertation topic:

1- Passion & Skills: Choose an AI subdomain that excites you and aligns with your skills (e.g., NLP, Computer Vision, Reinforcement Learning, etc.).

2- Career Impact: Select a topic relevant to your future career aspirations—whether in research, startups, or industry collaboration—as each path shapes different future opportunities.

3- Technology & Tools: Opt for topics that allow you to work with relevant, up-to-date technologies such as Machine Learning, Deep Learning, AutoML, Generative AI models, AI agents, etc.

4- Novelty & Relevance: Ensure your topic is current, impactful, and has the potential for originality.

5- Feasibility: Choose a project that is realistic given your available time and material resources.

6- Data Availability: Verify access to necessary data or establish a viable data collection strategy.

7- Application Area: Consider the real-world domain your project applies to—healthcare, finance, agriculture, cybersecurity, etc.—and ensure it is relevant and meaningful to both your interests and the field.

8- Scientific Contribution: Evaluate whether your research has the potential to advance AI knowledge, contribute to publications, or open doors to further academic opportunities.

9- Commercial & Industrial Potential: Assess whether your project could lead to a marketable product or attract industry interest.

10- Expert Insights: Conduct thorough research and seek advice from experienced professionals before finalizing your choice.

The point is that since you'll be dedicating several months to this project, it's important to select a topic that allows you to gain expertise, develop valuable skills, and achieve results that will benefit you beyond graduation.

More insights coming soon—stay tuned! 🚀
raw_content:
[First Guide: Choosing the Right AI Dissertation Topic]

We've compiled 10 criteria to help you choose the right AI dissertation topic:

1- Passion & Skills: Choose an AI subdomain that excites you and aligns with your skills (e.g., NLP, Computer Vision, Reinforcement Learning, etc.).

2- Career Impact: Select a topic relevant to your future career aspirations—whether in research, startups, or industry collaboration—as each path shapes different future opportunities.

3- Technology & Tools: Opt for topics that allow you to work with relevant, up-to-date technologies such as Machine Learning, Deep Learning, AutoML, Generative AI models, AI agents, etc.

4- Novelty & Relevance: Ensure your topic is current, impactful, and has the potential for originality.

5- Feasibility: Choose a project that is realistic given your available time and material resources.

6- Data Availability: Verify access to necessary data or establish a viable data collection strategy.

7- Application Area: Consider the real-world domain your project applies to—healthcare, finance, agriculture, cybersecurity, etc.—and ensure it is relevant and meaningful to both your interests and the field.

8- Scientific Contribution: Evaluate whether your research has the potential to advance AI knowledge, contribute to publications, or open doors to further academic opportunities.

9- Commercial & Industrial Potential: Assess whether your project could lead to a marketable product or attract industry interest.

10- Expert Insights: Conduct thorough research and seek advice from experienced professionals before finalizing your choice.

The point is that since you'll be dedicating several months to this project, it's important to select a topic that allows you to gain expertise, develop valuable skills, and achieve results that will benefit you beyond graduation.

More insights coming soon—stay tuned! 🚀

text:
[Platform to Connect Students with Research Teams]

Salam everyone,
As you know, students at our school currently lack a proper environment to explore and engage in scientific research.

To address this, a group of third-year students is developing a platform as part of their group project. The platform aims to:

- Connect students with research teams/professors at ENSIA and other schools of the scientific pole
- Discover available research topics
- Join research projects, get involved, and publish papers

This will help students interested in scientific research strengthen their CVs and research background.

Please take a moment to fill out this survey to help them better meet your needs: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe6jn5pRjvxu1nC5EEdiJZy7ah9ysHqhntEkhmnb88P1bQsqg/viewform Thank you for your support!

[Extracted PDF text]
Fiche technique du challange : Startup Factory 23/24/25/26 avril Au Centre universitaire de Tipaza Une collaboration entre l'incubateur privé CarpeDiem, le Centre universitaire de Tipaza via son incubateur CUTIN, et l'incubateur privé Suricate, sous le parrainage du Ministère des Startups, de l'Économie du Savoir et du Ministère de l'Enseignement Supérieur. Partenaires : • Ministère des Startups • Ministère de l'Enseignement Supérieur • CarpeDiem Concept • Centre universitaire de Tipaza (CUTIN) • Incubateur Suricate 1- Introduction L’innovation et l’entrepreneuriat sont au cœur du développement économique et technologique. Startup Factory 2025, initié et organisé par CarpeDiem Concept, s'inscrit dans cette dynamique en offrant un cadre structuré et opérationnel pour accompagner les entrepreneurs dans la transformation de leurs idées en startups viables et performantes. CarpeDiem Concept est bien plus qu’un incubateur : c’est un catalyseur d’innovation durable. Sa mission est d’accompagner les startups qui allient performance économique et impact positif en Algérie. 2- Carpe Diem Concept : Une Révolution dans la Création et la Gestion de Startups Le Carpe Diem Concept n'est pas qu'une simple approche entrepreneuriale, c'est une idéologie disruptive qui repense la création et la gestion des startups. Fondé sur l’agilité, la simplicité et l’efficacité, ce concept vise à maximiser la viabilité et la scalabilité des startups en adoptant une démarche pragmatique et orientée vers l'action. A- Les piliers du Carpe Diem Concept 1. Action immédiate et opportunité : Chaque idée viable doit être rapidement transformée en prototype concret (MVP), testé en conditions réelles pour une validation rapide. 2. Simplicité et efficacité : Réduction de la complexité grâce à une gestion minimaliste et performante, axée sur les besoins réels du marché. 3. Viabilité durable : L'accent est mis sur la création de valeur durable, avec des modèles économiques éprouvés et des stratégies de financement optimisées. 4. Impact et signification : Chaque startup doit viser un changement significatif, qu’il soit économique, social ou technologique. 3- Startup Factory : L’Usine à Startups du Carpe Diem Concept Le Startup Factory est bien plus qu'un simple espace d’innovation. C’est le catalyseur du Carpe Diem Concept, une véritable fabrique de startups où l'idéologie prend vie dans un environnement conçu pour transformer les idées en entreprises viables. - Un écosystème propice à la création Le Startup Factory repose sur un cadre structuré qui favorise la simplicité, l’efficacité et l’impact. Son objectif est d’accélérer le processus entrepreneurial en combinant Lean Management, agilité et pragmatisme, tout en restant ancré dans la réalité du marché. - Les fondements du Startup Factory 4- Objectif de l’événement Le Startup Factory a pour mission de former et accompagner les futurs entrepreneurs en leur fournissant les outils et l’environnement nécessaires pour structurer, tester et financer leurs projets. L’événement réunira : • Étudiants des grandes écoles • Experts, mentors et investisseurs • Institutions académiques et partenaires stratégiques Ensemble, nous favoriserons l’émergence de startups capables de répondre aux défis économiques et technologiques de demain. 5- Public Cible • Étudiants des écoles supérieures • Porteurs de projets disposant d’un Proof of Concept (POC) • Startups en phase d’amorçage cherchant à valider leur modèle • Anciens étudiants n’ayant pas encore concrétisé leurs projets Toutes les disciplines sont concernées : Tech, Industrie, Santé, Éducation, Énergie, Agroalimentaire… 6- Critères de sélection • Disposer d’un prototype ou d’un Proof of Concept (POC) • Avoir obtenu le Label Projet Innovant ou être en phase de labellisation • Présenter une idée innovante avec un fort potentiel de marché • Être motivé(e) et prêt(e) à travailler intensivement pendant 5 jours 7- Déroulement de l’événement Jour 1 : 23 avril - Lancement et structuration • Matin : o Cérémonie d’ouverture officielle. o Conférence : “Startups en Algérie : Opportunités et défis”. o Panel de discussion avec les parties prenantes de l’écosystème. • Après-midi : o Atelier : Structurer son idée avec le Lean Canvas. o Atelier : Définir une proposition de valeur unique. Jour 2 : 24 avril - Prototypage et innovation • Matin : o Atelier : Introduction au prototypage rapide avec la plateforme IBTIKAR. o Mentorat technique et validation des prototypes. o Atelier : Critères et processus pour obtenir le Label Startup. • Après-midi : o Atelier : Propriété intellectuelle et création juridique. Création juridique. o Atelier : Mécanismes de financement avec ASF et ANGEM. o Mentorat sur la viabilité économique et le modèle financier des projets. o Pitch. Jour 3: 25 avril - Excursion et team building • Matin : o Excursion à un site emblématique de Tipaza. o Activités de découverte culturelle et sociale. • Après-midi : Activités team building : o Jeu collaboratif : émersion dans la vie des startups présenté par l’incubateur international Suricate Lab. Activité – o Soirée : retour d’expérience - invité surprise - l’histoire d’une succès story en Algérie. Jour 4: 26 avril - Pitch final et clôture • Matin : o Compétition finale : Présentation des projets devant un jury stratégique. o Évaluation des critères : innovation, faisabilité, potentiel économique. • Après-midi : o Remise des prix : Labellisation et sélection des projets financés. o Cérémonie de clôture et networking avec partenaires et investisseurs. 8- Récompenses et opportunités • Accompagnement de l’obtention du label startup/ projet innovant. • Accès à des programmes d’incubation et d’accélération • Opportunités de financement avec des investisseurs et institutions partenaires • Mise en réseau avec des experts, mentors et industriels • Accompagnement pour la création de MVP. 9- Comment postuler ? - Inscrivez-vous dès maintenant via : https://surveyheart.com/form/67e06395adebc42de257e176 Contact & Infos : Startupfactory43@gmail.com - Ne ratez pas cette chance de concrétiser votre projet et de bâtir votre startup !

text:
[Context to navigate through the latest Advancement in AI]

Hi everyone,

We are trying to create a context for navigating the most important aspects related to our school (opportunities with companies, startups and incubator, scientific research, etc.). One critical point remains creating a non-academic context to navigate through the latest advances in AI (LLMs, Generative AI, AI agents, etc.).

We're launching a call for scientific clubs who would be interested in creating this context, and we will do our best to help and guide them with it. Interested clubs can brainstorm this further and contact us if they need any help.

Many thanks in advance!

text:
Dear all,
This is going to be a Q/A weekly post to answer your enquiries about CDE.
The post will remain pinned for one week.


---

Q1. What is the difference between the incubator and the CDE?

A1.

A.1.1. The University Facade

The incubator deals with startups (scalable business; unknown market and competitors).

The CDE deals with micro-enterprises (classical economic activities).


A.1.2. The Funding

The incubator connects you with partners who may finance you partially or totally.

The CDE refers you to NESDA (National Entrepreneurship Support and Development Agency), which offers tax free loans ranging from 500,000 to 10,000,000 DZD.

Stay tuned for next week post

text:
Q2
Should I be graduated to be funded by NESDA?
A2
NO.  You need to be BAC+1 day at university.
You need to participate in a 21 day training organised monthly by the nearest cde in your geographic location.

Please check www.cde.dz

To support collaboration and facilitate the formation of multidisciplinary teams ahead of the AgrI Challenge – Edition 2, we have prepared a shared Excel sheet: Link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1m-zLOyaeNggLsZfe8buLBw-GKFLr2DeTPtq29LDDoUU/edit?usp=sharing This sheet is a collaborative effort between students from ENSIA and ENSA and is shared with all the relevant communities. – Encourage balanced and complementary team composition.

Please use it respectfully and only for the intended purpose.

[Attached file: Internship Analysis.pdf]

[Extracted PDF text]
4th-Year Internship Feedback Analysis ⚠️ Important Disclaimer Please note: This analysis is based on voluntary feedback from students who chose to respond to the survey. Not all students who completed internships provided feedback, so this represents a subset of experiences. The insights presented here are subjective and based on individual student perspectives. Results should be interpreted with caution and should not be considered as definitive judgments about any institution or company. The goal is to provide helpful guidance for future students while respecting all parties involved. 55 Total Responses 20 Different Companies 4.1 Average Satisfaction 65% AI/Data Science Related 📊 Key Statistics Overall Satisfaction Distribution How Students Found Their Internships Internship Type Distribution Top Companies by Number of Interns 🏢 Company Insights Average Satisfaction by Company (Companies with 2+ interns) High-Satisfaction Companies SLB (Schlumberger) - Consistently high ratings (4-5/5) Strong mentorship and investment in interns Exposure to oil & gas industry with real data science projects Field trips to Hassi Messaoud for practical experience Excellent work culture and approachable staff ASAL (Algerian Space Agency) - Good technical exposure Multiple remote sensing projects and hackathons Research paper writing opportunities Exposure to GeoAI and satellite imagery processing Intensive but educational experience CRAAG - Excellent for research-oriented students AI applications in astronomy and astrophysics Access to international conferences and schools Research environment with experienced supervisors ⚠️ Common Challenges Finding Internships (67% found it difficult) Limited opportunities in hometowns (especially outside Algiers) Non-responsive applications Complex paperwork and documentation requirements Companies requesting longer commitments than available time Duration Issues One month perceived as too short for meaningful projects Difficulty in completing substantial work Limited time to build relationships and learn company culture Transportation Major concern for students, especially to distant locations Traffic and commute time affecting productivity Lack of transportation support from companies or school 🎯 Recommendations for Future Students 🔍 Finding an Internship Start Early: Begin searching 2-3 months before your intended start date Multiple Channels: Use school connections, teachers, and personal networking Prepare Documentation: Have all required papers ready in advance Follow Up: Don't hesitate to follow up on applications Consider Remote Options: Some companies offer hybrid or remote internships 💼 During the Internship Be Proactive: Ask questions and seek additional learning opportunities Network: Build relationships with colleagues and supervisors Document Everything: Keep track of projects and learnings for future reference Adapt Quickly: Be flexible with changing requirements and priorities Seek Feedback: Regular check-ins with supervisors improve the experience 🎓 Maximizing Learning Practical over Discovery: Practical internships generally provide higher satisfaction AI Relevance: Ensure the internship aligns with your AI/Data Science goals Real Projects: Look for opportunities to work on actual company projects Research Opportunities: Consider internships that offer research paper writing Industry Exposure: Value internships that provide sector-specific knowledge 📈 Satisfaction Factors Factors Contributing to High Satisfaction Quality Supervision: Accessible, knowledgeable supervisors significantly impact satisfaction Real Projects: Working on actual company problems rather than theoretical exercises Learning Environment: Companies that invest in intern education and development Work Culture: Open, supportive environments where interns feel valued Industry Exposure: Opportunities to understand the business context and applications Skill Development: Chances to learn new technologies and methodologies 🌟 Success Stories Notable Achievements Research Publications: Several students contributed to research papers during their internships Hackathon Participation: Multiple students participated in company-organized hackathons Real-World Impact: Projects like fuel sales prediction, lithology classification, and intrusion detection Cross-Disciplinary Learning: Exposure to domains like astronomy, geology, and renewable energy Professional Networks: Building connections for future career opportunities This analysis is based on feedback from 55 students who completed 4th-year internships. The insights are intended to help future students make informed decisions about their internship choices. Data analysis conducted to support student success and improve internship experiences.

text:
Hi everyone,

Since one of the issues raised in the internship feedback was not getting replies from companies,

We recommend checking this previous message and trying LinkedIn as an alternative, which is often faster and more effective than emails or in-person visits.

- Search for the company on LinkedIn
- Go to the "People" section
- Find someone in a relevant role
- Send a brief message explaining your motivation, include your CV and GitHub link

That person might be directly involved in moving things forward or could transmit your message to the right person.
We hope this helps!

text:
Estimated Career Path Preferences for ENSIA Students

Ranking from Most to Least Preferred:
👨‍💼Work as engineer in a company — 34.7%
🚀Start a company or startup — 22.9%
🧪Scientific research / PhD — 19.5%
💻Freelancer — 16.9%
📊Consultant — 5.9%

Note:
- One survey was formal, the other informal (in class).
- Total participants: 70 students.
- Results are based on a sample and not fully representative.
- They offer a general idea and should be viewed with caution.
- Preferences may shift over time as students gain experience.

Salam all,

Wishing you good luck
raw_content:
Salam all, 

Here are some recommendations based on my own experience for international summer/internship program applications.

Conclusion: The clearer your application and your understanding of the program, the host, your goals, and how the program fits your path, the more it will stand out.

Wishing you good luck
[Contains image]

[Extracted image text]
Tips for Applying to Summer/Internship Programs e Research the host organization well Know their mission and activitiies. Show genuine interest — not just a copy-paste application. e Prepare a clear work plan Align it with the host's goals. Show how your background and motivation bring value. e Polish your CV, GitHub, and portfolio Highlight relevant projects. Make everything easy to read and professional. e Mention any contact at the host org If someone agreed to work with you, say it clearly — it boosts your chances. ¢ Be strategic in your choices Avoid oversubscribed cities/projects. Less-known ones may give you better odds. e¢ Communicate clearly Be focused, structured, and concise. Make your plan easy to understand. e Expect rejections — and learn from them Each try improves your experience and future applications.

📢 Announcement: AIIA’25 – First International Conference on AI & Innovative Applications

We are pleased to announce AIIA’25, taking place December 2–3, 2025 at University M'Hamed Bougara, Boumerdes, Algeria.

AIIA’25 brings together researchers, industry leaders, and startups to share cutting-edge AI research and real-world applications, with a unique focus on bridging academia and entrepreneurship.

Key Dates

Submission Deadline: Sept 1, 2025
Acceptance Notification: Sept 20, 2025
Camera-Ready: Nov 1, 2025

Tracks:
AI research
Industry applications
Startup innovation

Accepted papers will appear in IEEE Xplore.
Details & submission: aiia.univ-boumerdes.dz Contact: aiia25@univ-boumerdes.dz | +213 552 78 80 41

We look forward to seeing you in Boumerdes!

Key Dates

We look forward to seeing you in Boumerdes!

text:
💡 FYP Research Tip

If your Final Year Project (FYP) is research-oriented, one of the best ways to quickly get an overview of a field is by reading recent "Survey" & "Review" papers.

✅ They summarize the latest progress in the area
✅ Compare existing approaches & methods
✅ Highlight limitations in current work
✅ Point out open problems and future research directions

👉 Pro tip: Look for surveys published in the last 2–3 years in reputable journals/conferences to stay up to date.

📚 Note: Books from reputed publishers (textbooks/handbooks) are also valuable, they can give you fundamental theory, background knowledge, and standard methods in a structured way.

text:
Hi all, we mentioned many possible outcomes during your curriculum: apps, patents, startups, etc. But, we never mentioned that collecting a dataset can also be a valuable outcome.

✨ Why it matters:
- Work on real-world projects, especially in the Algerian context
- Face less competition with a unique dataset
- Gain recognition if shared publicly

🏅 Recognition you might get:
- Citations in research papers
- Acknowledgments from labs or companies
- Invitations to collaborate on the dataset

📚 Here’s a resource detailing the process of data collection, particularly for machine learning: https://www.altexsoft.com/blog/data-collection-machine-learning/

text:
📚 Accessing Research Papers Online

If you need to access research papers for your research or final-year projects, here are two simple options:

1️⃣ SNDL Access – Create an account through our university library to access a wide range of academic journals and databases.

2️⃣ Alternative Option – You can also use Sci-Hub to access papers directly when institutional access isn’t available.

text:
🚀 Startup vs. Micro-Entreprise — What’s the Difference?

If you’re hesitating between joining an incubator or the Centre de Développement de l’Entrepreneuriat (CDE), here’s the key idea 👇

Startup (Incubator): Focused on innovation and rapid growth — ideal if you have a new tech idea and aim to scale big.

Micro-Entreprise (CDE): Focused on stability and self-employment — perfect for small, local businesses or service-based projects.

Both paths are valuable — choose depending on whether you want to innovate and grow fast, or build a stable small business 💡
